name: Squadcast Incident Management API
what_it_does: PagerDuty-alternative incident management and on-call scheduling platform with REST API — providing alert routing, on-call schedules, escalation policies, incident lifecycle management, and service reliability reporting for SRE teams.
use_cases: Creating and routing incidents programmatically from monitoring tools via API
Querying on-call schedules to determine current on-call engineer
Managing incident lifecycle (acknowledge, resolve, reassign) via API automation
Syncing incident data with ITSM tools (Jira, ServiceNow) via webhook
Generating reliability metrics (MTTA, MTTR) reports via API for SRE review
not_for: Simple email alerting without on-call scheduling
Customer-facing status pages without incident management context
Enterprise contact center with complex call routing
best_when: An SRE or DevOps team needs affordable PagerDuty alternative with full incident management API, on-call scheduling, and good monitoring tool integrations.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-grade incident management with complex ITSM workflows or advanced analytics beyond basic reliability metrics.

auth.notes: API token from account settings. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Webhook for incident lifecycle events. Refresh tokens not required — long-lived API tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 5 users, basic features
pricing.notes: Free tier for small teams. Affordable vs PagerDuty. Indian company (Bangalore). Strong Datadog, Grafana, Prometheus integration. Good API documentation. SOC2 certified. Growing SRE community adoption as PagerDuty alternative.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for incidents, services, escalation policies, on-call schedules, and users. Bearer token auth. Webhook for incident events. Good documentation for core entities. Webhook payload structure well-documented. API stable across versions.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Incident creation creates new incidents. Incident resolution and acknowledgment idempotent (safe to retry). Service management idempotent by ID. On-call reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Alert routing depends on service configuration — services must be set up before incidents route correctly
On-call schedules use complex rotation logic — query current on-call via dedicated endpoint
Incident deduplication requires routing key usage — must implement in alert source integration
Indian company — support primarily in IST timezone

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. Indian company with US and EU data residency options. Bearer token auth — no scope granularity. Standard SaaS security for incident management.
